Title: Register a new pregnancy episode and enroll patient into the Antenatal Care program
Preconditions: Logged-in clinician with Manage Programs privilege; female patient of childbearing age exists
Test_Steps: 1. Open patient dashboard | 2. Start the Antenatal Care program enrollment | 3. Set enrollment date and outcome state | 4. Save enrollment
Expected_Result: Patient is enrolled in the ANC program, current state shows Active, enrollment appears under Care/Programs with correct date and provider

Title: Prevent duplicate active ANC enrollment for the same patient
Preconditions: Patient already has an active ANC program enrollment
Test_Steps: 1. Open the patient already enrolled in ANC | 2. Attempt to start a second concurrent ANC enrollment | 3. Save
Expected_Result: System blocks or warns against a second overlapping active enrollment; no duplicate active episode is created

Title: Reject ANC enrollment for a male patient
Test_Steps: 1. Open a male patient | 2. Attempt to enroll into ANC program | 3. Observe validation
Expected_Result: Enrollment is rejected or flagged as clinically inappropriate; appropriate warning is shown

Title: Capture LMP at registration and validate it is not a future date
Preconditions: Clinician enrolling a patient into ANC
Test_Steps: 1. Begin ANC registration | 2. Enter LMP date in the future | 3. Attempt to save
Expected_Result: System rejects a future LMP with a validation message; record is not saved

Title: Capture gravida and parity obstetric history
Test_Steps: 1. Open ANC history form | 2. Enter gravida, parity, abortions, living children | 3. Save
Expected_Result: Obstetric history is saved and displayed; values are internally consistent (para+abortions <= gravida-1 for current pregnancy)

Title: Reject obstetric history where parity exceeds gravida
Test_Steps: 1. Enter gravida lower than parity | 2. Save
Expected_Result: Validation rejects inconsistent obstetric history; error explains gravida must be >= prior births

Title: Calculate EDD from LMP using Naegele rule (LMP + 280 days)
Test_Steps: 1. Enter LMP | 2. Trigger EDD calculation | 3. Verify computed EDD
Expected_Result: EDD is computed as LMP + 280 days (2026-10-08) and displayed on the ANC form

Title: EDD calculation across a leap-year boundary
Preconditions: Patient with LMP near year/leap boundary
Test_Steps: 1. Enter LMP spanning Feb 29 in calculation window | 2. Compute EDD
Expected_Result: EDD accounts for leap day (Feb 29) correctly; computed date is consistent with 280-day addition

Title: Gestational age computed from LMP at current visit
Preconditions: Patient enrolled with known LMP; current date known
Test_Steps: 1. Open ANC visit | 2. View computed GA in weeks+days
Expected_Result: GA is displayed as completed weeks and days (13w0d) matching (VisitDate-LMP)

Title: Reconcile EDD from ultrasound vs LMP when discrepancy exceeds threshold
Preconditions: Both LMP-based EDD and ultrasound EDD available
Test_Steps: 1. Enter LMP EDD | 2. Enter ultrasound EDD/CRL dating | 3. System evaluates discrepancy | 4. Apply dating rule
Expected_Result: When discrepancy exceeds the dating rule threshold (e.g., >7d in 1st trimester), ultrasound dating is preferred and flagged; otherwise LMP retained

Title: GA boundary at term and post-term classification
Preconditions: Patient with computed GA near term
Test_Steps: 1. Set GA to 36w6d, 37w0d, 41w6d, 42w0d via dated LMP | 2. Observe term classification
Test_Data: GA values: 36w6d preterm; 37w0d term; 41w6d term; 42w0d post-term
Expected_Result: Classification transitions correctly: <37w preterm, 37-41w6d term, >=42w post-term

Title: High-risk pregnancy flag triggered by advanced maternal age
Preconditions: Patient enrolled in ANC with recorded age
Test_Steps: 1. Open risk assessment | 2. Enter maternal age >=35 | 3. Evaluate risk
Expected_Result: High-risk flag is set for advanced maternal age and surfaced prominently on the dashboard

Title: Risk decision table across multiple concurrent risk factors
Test_Steps: 1. Enter combinations of age, prior C-section, hypertension, multiple gestation | 2. Evaluate composite risk
Test_Data: Combo A: Age40+PriorCS=High; Combo B: HTN+Twins=High; Combo C: none=Low
Expected_Result: Composite risk level is computed per decision table; any qualifying factor yields High-risk; no factor yields Low

Title: Rh-negative mother flagged for anti-D prophylaxis
Preconditions: ANC patient with blood group recorded
Test_Steps: 1. Record maternal blood group Rh-negative | 2. Evaluate alerts
Expected_Result: System flags Rh-negative status and recommends anti-D prophylaxis assessment; alert is recorded

Title: Pre-eclampsia early-warning flag from elevated BP and proteinuria
Preconditions: ANC visit with vitals and urinalysis captured
Test_Steps: 1. Enter BP >=140/90 | 2. Enter proteinuria positive | 3. Evaluate alert
Expected_Result: Pre-eclampsia warning is raised when BP threshold and proteinuria both met; escalation guidance is shown

Title: No false high-risk flag when all parameters normal
Preconditions: ANC visit with all normal parameters
Test_Steps: 1. Enter normal age, BP, history | 2. Evaluate risk
Expected_Result: Risk remains Low; no high-risk flag is raised

Title: Generate WHO-aligned ANC visit schedule from EDD
Test_Steps: 1. Confirm EDD | 2. Generate recommended ANC contact schedule | 3. Review scheduled visits
Expected_Result: Recommended contact dates are generated aligned to gestational milestones; visits appear on the schedule

Title: Flag a missed ANC visit and compute overdue status
Preconditions: Patient has a scheduled ANC visit in the past not completed
Test_Steps: 1. Advance to date after scheduled visit | 2. View appointment status
Expected_Result: Visit transitions from Scheduled to Missed/Overdue; defaulter tracking surfaces the patient

Title: Reschedule an ANC visit and verify state transition
Test_Steps: 1. Open scheduled ANC appointment | 2. Reschedule to a new date | 3. Save
Expected_Result: Appointment status transitions Scheduled to Rescheduled; new date persists; history retains original

Title: Pairwise coverage of visit type, trimester and provider role
Test_Steps: 1. Create visits varying VisitType x Trimester x ProviderRole | 2. Verify each combination saves
Expected_Result: Each pairwise combination is accepted and persisted with correct attributes

Title: Record routine ANC visit observations (fundal height, FHR, presentation)
Test_Steps: 1. Open ANC visit form | 2. Enter fundal height, fetal heart rate, presentation, fetal movements | 3. Save
Expected_Result: Observations are saved and shown under the encounter with correct units and datetime

Title: Boundary validation on fetal heart rate range
Test_Steps: 1. Enter FHR below and above plausible range | 2. Save
Expected_Result: Out-of-range FHR (50, 220) triggers validation/alert; normal range values accepted

Title: Symphysis-fundal height vs gestational age discrepancy alert
Test_Steps: 1. Enter SFH | 2. Compare to GA in weeks | 3. Evaluate discrepancy
Expected_Result: Discrepancy >3cm between SFH and GA raises a growth-concern flag for further assessment

Title: Record tetanus toxoid immunization during ANC
Test_Steps: 1. Open immunization section | 2. Record TT dose with date | 3. Save
Expected_Result: TT immunization is recorded with dose number and date; immunization history updated

Title: Order ANC profile labs and link results to pregnancy episode
Preconditions: ANC visit active; lab module enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Order ANC profile (Hb, blood group, HIV, syphilis, urine) | 2. Enter results | 3. Verify linkage
Expected_Result: Lab orders are created and results link to the ANC episode; abnormal Hb flagged as anemia

Title: Anemia severity classification from hemoglobin
Test_Steps: 1. Enter Hb values | 2. Evaluate anemia classification
Expected_Result: Hb is classified per WHO thresholds (>=11 normal, 10-10.9 mild, 7-9.9 moderate, <7 severe); severe triggers urgent flag

Title: Gestational diabetes screening result interpretation
Test_Steps: 1. Enter fasting and 2-hr glucose | 2. Evaluate GDM criteria
Expected_Result: GDM diagnosis flagged when any threshold met; result interpreted and recorded

Title: Record a normal vaginal delivery outcome and transition ANC state
Preconditions: Patient in active ANC program reaching delivery
Test_Steps: 1. Open delivery/L&D form | 2. Record delivery mode, datetime, outcome | 3. Transition ANC program state to Delivered
Expected_Result: Delivery is recorded; ANC program state transitions Active to Delivered; postnatal pathway becomes available

Title: Record cesarean section with indication
Test_Steps: 1. Open delivery form | 2. Select mode=Cesarean | 3. Enter indication and anesthesia | 4. Save
Expected_Result: CS delivery is recorded with indication; mode-specific fields are captured and persisted

Title: Reject delivery datetime earlier than pregnancy start (LMP)
Test_Steps: 1. Open delivery form | 2. Enter delivery datetime before LMP | 3. Save
Expected_Result: Validation rejects impossible delivery date preceding conception window

Title: Capture estimated blood loss and trigger PPH alert at threshold
Test_Steps: 1. Enter estimated blood loss | 2. Evaluate PPH threshold
Expected_Result: Blood loss >=500ml (vaginal) raises postpartum hemorrhage alert; below threshold no alert

Title: Record multiple birth (twins) with per-baby outcomes
Test_Steps: 1. Set number of babies=2 | 2. Record outcome per baby | 3. Save
Expected_Result: Each baby has independent outcome, sex and weight; two newborn records can be linked

Title: Record stillbirth outcome and suppress newborn registration prompt
Test_Steps: 1. Record delivery outcome=Stillbirth | 2. Observe downstream workflow
Expected_Result: Stillbirth is recorded; newborn live-registration is not prompted; bereavement/followup pathway available

Title: Register newborn and link to mother as relationship
Test_Steps: 1. From delivery record create newborn patient | 2. Capture sex, weight, APGAR | 3. Establish mother-child relationship
Expected_Result: Newborn patient is created and linked to mother via Parent/Child relationship; both records cross-reference

Title: Low APGAR score triggers neonatal resuscitation flag
Test_Steps: 1. Enter APGAR at 1 and 5 minutes | 2. Evaluate flag
Expected_Result: Low APGAR (<7) raises neonatal distress/resuscitation flag and prompts escalation

Title: Low birth weight classification and follow-up flag
Test_Steps: 1. Enter birth weight values | 2. Evaluate classification
Expected_Result: Birth weight classified (LBW<2.5kg, VLBW<1.5kg, ELBW<1.0kg); LBW flags special care follow-up

Title: Prevent linking newborn to a non-existent mother record
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt to create mother-child relationship with invalid mother UUID | 2. Save
Expected_Result: System rejects the link; relationship is not created; error is shown

Title: Enroll mother into postnatal care after delivery
Test_Steps: 1. Open postnatal program enrollment | 2. Set PNC start date | 3. Save
Expected_Result: Mother is enrolled in postnatal program; PNC visit schedule (e.g., day 3, day 7, week 6) becomes available

Title: Record postnatal danger signs and escalate
Test_Steps: 1. Open PNC visit | 2. Record danger signs (fever, heavy bleeding, foul lochia) | 3. Evaluate escalation
Expected_Result: Presence of any postnatal danger sign triggers urgent referral flag and is recorded

Title: Record exclusive breastfeeding counseling and newborn feeding
Preconditions: PNC visit active with linked newborn
Test_Steps: 1. Open feeding section | 2. Record feeding method and counseling provided | 3. Save
Expected_Result: Feeding method and counseling are recorded against the PNC encounter

Title: Postpartum family planning method selection
Test_Steps: 1. Open family planning section | 2. Record method chosen and start date | 3. Save
Expected_Result: Selected contraceptive method is recorded with start date in the postnatal record

Title: Plot cervical dilation on partograph and detect alert-line crossing
Preconditions: Patient in active labor; partograph available (assumption: labor-monitoring module/form)
Test_Steps: 1. Open partograph | 2. Plot serial cervical dilation vs time | 3. Evaluate alert/action line
Expected_Result: Plotting right of the alert line raises a labor-progress alert; crossing action line escalates intervention

Title: Record fetal heart rate trend on partograph and flag bradycardia
Test_Steps: 1. Plot FHR at intervals | 2. Evaluate for sustained abnormality
Expected_Result: Sustained FHR <110 (bradycardia) is flagged on the partograph for fetal-distress evaluation

Title: Reject partograph entry with non-monotonic timeline
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt to add a reading with timestamp earlier than the last plotted point | 2. Save
Expected_Result: Out-of-order timeline entry is rejected or reordered; data integrity of the labor curve is preserved

Title: Gestational age boundary at exactly 0 weeks and at 43 weeks
Preconditions: Patient with LMP equal to or far before current date
Test_Steps: 1. Set LMP=current date (GA 0w0d) | 2. Set LMP 301 days prior (GA 43w) | 3. Observe handling
Expected_Result: GA of 0w0d is accepted as minimum; >42w surfaces post-term warning; no negative GA permitted

Title: Reject negative gestational age when visit precedes LMP
Test_Steps: 1. Backdate a visit before LMP | 2. View computed GA
Expected_Result: System prevents negative GA; visit date earlier than LMP is rejected or GA shown as invalid

Title: Expose pregnancy status and EDD via FHIR observation/condition
Preconditions: FHIR API enabled; ANC episode exists
Test_Steps: 1. GET FHIR Condition/Observation for pregnancy status | 2. Verify coding and EDD value
Expected_Result: FHIR resource returns pregnancy status with correct LOINC/SNOMED codes and EDD date value

Title: Pregnancy episode represented as FHIR EpisodeOfCare / CarePlan
Preconditions: FHIR API enabled; ANC program enrollment exists
Test_Steps: 1. GET EpisodeOfCare or CarePlan for the ANC enrollment | 2. Validate status and period
Expected_Result: Resource reflects active episode with correct period and links to Patient; mapping is consistent

Title: Validate FHIR observation rejects invalid GA unit
Test_Steps: 1. POST Observation for GA with invalid UCUM unit | 2. Observe response
Expected_Result: Server rejects invalid UCUM unit with OperationOutcome error; valid units (wk) accepted

Title: ADT message links newborn to mother via next-of-kin/parent segment
Test_Steps: 1. Send ADT^A28 for newborn with parent linkage in NK1 | 2. Verify processing
Expected_Result: Newborn registration message processes and establishes mother linkage; ACK returned

Title: ORU message delivers ANC lab result to pregnancy episode
Test_Steps: 1. Send ORU^R01 with Hb result and pregnancy context | 2. Verify result links to ANC episode
Expected_Result: Result is filed against the patient and surfaces under ANC; anemia flag derived where applicable

Title: REST create ANC program enrollment via API
Preconditions: REST API enabled; auth token available
Test_Steps: 1. POST programenrollment for ANC | 2. GET to confirm | 3. Validate state
Expected_Result: Enrollment is created via API with HTTP 201; GET returns active state and correct dates

Title: API rejects ANC enrollment with malformed date
Test_Steps: 1. POST enrollment with invalid date format | 2. Observe response
Expected_Result: API returns 400 with validation error; no enrollment created

Title: Verify referential integrity of mother-child relationship at DB level
Preconditions: DB read access (test env); mother-child link created
Test_Steps: 1. Query relationship table | 2. Verify person_a/person_b and relationship type | 3. Verify FK integrity
Test_Data: Relationship=Parent/Child; mother and newborn person ids
Expected_Result: Relationship row exists with valid foreign keys to both persons; voided flag not set

Title: Verify EDD observation persisted with correct concept and value datatype
Preconditions: DB read access (test env); EDD recorded
Test_Steps: 1. Query obs table for EDD concept | 2. Verify value_datetime populated
Expected_Result: EDD obs persists in value_datetime (not value_text); concept id maps to EDD; not voided

Title: PHI access to maternity record is audit-logged
Preconditions: Audit logging enabled; ANC record exists
Test_Steps: 1. Access a maternal patient record | 2. Inspect audit log
Expected_Result: Access event is captured with user, timestamp, patient and action for the maternity PHI

Title: Unauthorized role cannot view or edit maternity clinical data
Test_Steps: 1. Log in as low-privilege user | 2. Attempt to open ANC clinical forms | 3. Attempt to edit
Expected_Result: Access is denied per RBAC; clinical maternity data is not exposed or editable

Title: Prevent stored XSS in ANC free-text complaint field
Preconditions: ANC visit form with free-text field
Test_Steps: 1. Enter script payload in complaints field | 2. Save and reload record
Expected_Result: Input is sanitized/escaped; no script executes on render; stored value is neutralized

Title: ANC visit form is keyboard navigable and screen-reader labeled
Preconditions: ANC form rendered; assistive tech available
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate all fields via keyboard (Tab/Shift+Tab) | 2. Verify labels announced | 3. Verify focus order
Expected_Result: All inputs are reachable by keyboard with associated labels and logical focus order; errors announced

Title: Risk/alert flags meet color-contrast and non-color cues
Test_Steps: 1. Inspect alert styling | 2. Verify contrast ratio | 3. Verify icon/text accompanies color
Expected_Result: Risk alerts use sufficient contrast and a non-color cue (icon/text), not color alone

Title: Exploratory session on concurrent edits to the same ANC visit
Preconditions: Two clinicians can access same patient visit
Test_Steps: 1. Open same ANC visit in two sessions | 2. Edit overlapping observations | 3. Save both | 4. Observe conflict handling
Test_Data: Session A SFH=24; Session B SFH=26
Expected_Result: Concurrency is handled without silent data loss; last-write/lock/merge behavior is observed and consistent

Title: Exploratory boundary probing of EDD/GA with timezone and DST changes
Preconditions: Server/client in differing timezones; DST transition in window
Test_Steps: 1. Record LMP near DST change | 2. Compute GA/EDD across timezone difference | 3. Compare
Test_Data: LMP near DST boundary; client TZ != server TZ
Expected_Result: GA/EDD remain consistent and date-correct across timezone/DST; no off-by-one day errors

Title: Defaulter list correctly aggregates overdue ANC patients
Preconditions: Multiple ANC patients with mixed visit statuses
Test_Steps: 1. Generate ANC defaulter report | 2. Verify only overdue/missed patients listed
Test_Data: Patients with overdue, completed, upcoming visits
Expected_Result: Report lists only patients with missed/overdue visits; completed and upcoming are excluded

Title: Partograph-to-delivery state transition continuity
Test_Steps: 1. Complete labor monitoring | 2. Record delivery | 3. Verify ANC->Delivered->PNC chain
Expected_Result: End-to-end state chain transitions cleanly with no orphaned episode; all linked records consistent

Title: Pairwise risk-factor and gestational-stage alert presentation
Test_Steps: 1. Combine RiskFactor x Trimester x AlertChannel | 2. Verify alert raised and routed
Expected_Result: Each pairwise combination raises and routes the correct alert without suppression or duplication
